articles.title: Main Vaapas Aaunga BO Day 8: Diljit starrer hits Rs 26 cr worldwide
articles.description: Imtiaz Ali's poignant partition drama 'Main Vaapas Aaunga', featuring Diljit Dosanjh, has impressively surpassed the Rs 14 crore milestone in net collections in India by the eighth day. On its second Friday, the film collected Rs 1.90 crore, with a new total domestic accumulation reaching Rs 16.88 crore. Internationally, it has achieved approximately Rs 26.13 crore amidst fresh competition from 'Cocktail 2'. Read on to know more in detail.

articles.title: Hailee Steinfeld and Josh Allen finally reveal baby daughter's name two months after keeping fans in suspense
articles.description: Hailee Steinfeld publicly revealed the name of her daughter, Harper Haize Allen, through a heartfelt letter published on her Substack newsletter Beau Society on June 19. In the letter, addressed directly to Harper, Steinfeld reflects on early motherhood with rawness and warmth, describing how time now moves in ounces and naps rather than months. She and husband Josh Allen welcomed Harper on April 2, less than a year after their Santa Barbara wedding in May 2025.

articles.title: 'Nooru Saami' opens with Rs 75 lakh India Net
articles.description: Director Sasi's 'Nooru Saami,' starring Swasika and Vijay Antony, has opened to a modest Rs 75 lakh nett on its first day. The emotional family drama, exploring a mother's desire to remarry and her sons' resistance, saw a 15 percent overall occupancy. While morning shows were slow, night shows indicated a gradual improvement in audience interest for this poignant narrative.

articles.title: ‘Tyrant’ shoot paused following shooting at World Cup watch party
articles.description: Filming for the thriller 'Tyrant', featuring Demi Moore and Charlize Theron, was halted in Los Angeles after a shooting near a World Cup watch party. Cast and crew were sent home as a precaution. The incident occurred in Koreatown during a large gathering. Production decided to pause work despite no official evacuation order, a move that proved wise as police later issued a citywide alert for crowd control.

The film opened with a decent Rs 13.50 crore collection, with occupancy improving throughout the day. Read on to know about the streaming details of Cocktail 2.

Smith's journey highlights how proactive habits, not luck, pave the way for success across all life's endeavors.

articles.title: Inside Preity Zinta’s Los Angeles 'ghar ki kheti': How the 'Batwara 1947' star's kitchen garden keeps her connected to her Himachali roots
articles.description: Actress Preity Zinta has found a grounding passion in Los Angeles: cultivating her own kitchen garden, affectionately termed 'ghar ki kheti'. Inspired by her mother and her Himachal Pradesh roots, Zinta's pandemic-born hobby now yields organic produce like apples, tomatoes, and chillies, bringing her immense joy and a connection to home.

articles.title: Proverb of the day: ‘When a clown moves into a palace, he does not become a king; the palace becomes a circus’, a lesson on why titles don’t define people
articles.description: A popular saying highlights how placing the wrong person in charge can transform an institution into chaos. It emphasizes that titles do not guarantee competence. True leadership stems from character and responsibility, not just a position. This timeless wisdom warns against judging individuals by their status alone. Actions and integrity are what truly matter for an organization's success.

articles.title: Burned Haystack: Gen Zs Are Done Searching For 'The One' And It's Working
articles.description: Tired of looking for the perfect partner? Gen Z has a new plan: the 'Burned Haystack' trend. It's not about finding a needle in a haystack anymore. The new rule is to just burn the whole thing down. This means dropping impossible standards and dating app drama to find someone real.
